input.headline: Why ‘walkaway wives’ are leading the charge in midlife divorce

raw.headline: Why ‘walkaway wives’ are leading the charge in midlife divorce

winner: After the divorce, she took the "walkaway" title so literally that she refused to enter any room without first performing a high-speed power walk through the front door.

candidates.text: Midlife divorce? Please—my wife left me for a man who *stays* in bed longer than I do. She called him 'low-maintenance.' I call him my alimony.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with a specific, relatable scenario and clever wordplay on 'alimony'"}

candidates.text: Why are husbands suddenly so good at yoga? Because they're trying to stay flexible during their wives' midlife divorces.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks specificity and surprise. The connection between yoga and midlife divorce feels forced and generic."}

candidates.text: Why did the treadmill get divorced? It couldn't handle the walkaway wives anymore.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks clarity and surprise; the connection between treadmills and 'walkaway wives' is weak and not immediately funny."}

candidates.text: After the divorce, she took the "walkaway" title so literally that she refused to enter any room without first performing a high-speed power walk through the front door.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with a specific, visual gag that ties back to the title in a clever way."}

candidates.text: They aren't just leaving the marriage; they're taking the "walk" out of the "walkway.

My wife finally decided to "walk away" from our marriage, which was a relief because I was starting to suspect she was just trying to escape the fact that I’m the one who actually knows how to work the thermostat.
I told my wife I was done with marriage, she said, "Are you serious?" I said, "No, I'm just walking away.

I told my wife I needed space, and she said, "Let's go for a walk." I said, "No, we're doing the 'walk-away' thing now." She looked at me like I just suggested we try skydiving with a paper bag helmet.
I tried to convince my wife to take a walk, and now I'm the one walking away from the marriage.
